steps.title: Extract center and radius of circle P
steps.content:
The equation $(x-3)^{2}+(y+7)^{2}=25$ is in standard form $(x-h)^{2}+(y-k)^{2}=r^{2}$, where the center is $(h, k)$ and radius is $r$.
From circle P's equation: center is $(3, -7)$ and radius is $r = \sqrt{25} = 5$.

steps.title: Find center and radius of circle Q
steps.content:
Circle Q's center is 1 unit to the right and 2 units below circle P's center:
$$\text{New center: } (3+1, -7-2) = (4, -9)$$Circle Q's diameter is double that of circle P. Since P's diameter is $2 \cdot 5 = 10$, circle Q's diameter is $2 \cdot 10 = 20$, giving radius $r_Q = 10$.

steps.title: Write circle Q's equation
steps.content:
With center $(4, -9)$ and radius \$10$, the equation of circle Q is:
$$(x-4)^{2}+(y+9)^{2}=100$$That matches choice D — D is correct.
